mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment above a structural threshold signals significant slack in the economy, depressing aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations. This leads to negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon as growth forecasts are revised downwards.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE is 9.0%, well above historical full-employment levels.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 8.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A near-zero federal funds rate provides a powerful and continuous tailwind for equities by lowering the discount rate for future cash flows and encouraging portfolio shifts into riskier assets. This effect persists as long as the central bank maintains its accommodative stance.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS is 0.17%, indicating a Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) is in effect.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return of the S&P 500 is not statistically significantly positive (p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month growth in retail sales is a direct signal of strengthening consumer demand, which constitutes the largest part of the US economy. This leads to upward revisions in revenue forecasts and drives positive short-term equity performance.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of RSAFS 3-month change on 20-day forward returns fails to remain positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously accelerating industrial production and rising consumer prices indicate that demand growth is outstripping the economy's productive capacity. This 'overheating' scenario compresses profit margins and increases the probability of monetary tightening, causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY % change > 4% AND CPIAUCSL YoY % change > 2%
failure_condition: In periods where both INDPRO and CPIAUCSL are accelerating year-over-year, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary environment with high unemployment, strong nonfarm payrolls growth is a primary confirmation signal of a durable economic recovery. This reduces uncertainty and risk premia, driving equity prices higher over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:The economy is emerging from the 2008 financial crisis.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return following PAYEMS prints above 150k is not statistically different from the average return following prints below 150k when UNRATE is above 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between strong headline GDP growth and persistently weak housing starts indicates an imbalanced and potentially unsustainable recovery. Weakness in the highly cyclical housing sector signals poor household formation and credit conditions, acting as a leading indicator of a future slowdown that negatively impacts equities.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 YoY % change > 2.5% AND HOUST 3m avg < 600
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST remains at historically depressed levels post-2008.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of quarters exhibiting this GDP-Housing divergence.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation breaches the implicit central bank target, it triggers market expectations of imminent monetary tightening. This leads to an immediate repricing of risk assets, causing negative returns over a short horizon as discount rates adjust upwards.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following an inflation report with YoY CPIAUCSL > 2.0% is not statistically significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained momentum in industrial production is a direct measure of the health of the economy's supply side (manufacturing, mining, utilities). Positive momentum reflects robust business activity and improving corporate fundamentals, which supports higher equity prices over the medium term.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 6-month change in INDPRO and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over a 5-year rolling window.
